localAlgorithmPriority: External references sharpen NHS App integration, webview constraints, accessibility, and Playwright proof technique; the local blueprints remain authoritative.

references.label: NHS App web integration
references.borrowed: Readiness distinguishes responsive-web integration, Sandpit/AOS progression, SCAL evidence, limited release, monthly data, annual assurance, incidents, and journey-change notice.
references.rejected: The repository does not claim live NHS App approval from local contract evidence alone.

references.label: NHS App developer web integration guidance
references.borrowed: Webview limitations, header handling, NHS App traffic detection, and SSO query-token scrubbing remain active route-coverage concerns.
references.rejected: Raw asserted-login identity values are never placed in fixtures, UI, traces, or generated reports.

references.label: NHS App developer web integration overview
references.borrowed: The NHS App channel is treated as a hosted webview over the existing patient journeys, not as a separate product fork.
references.rejected: No UI client is allowed to infer channel authority from user-agent or route behavior alone.

references.label: NHS App accessibility statement
references.borrowed: The UI checks cover keyboard, screen-reader structure, 200 percent zoom expectations, mobile/browser use, and WCAG 2.2 AA alignment.
references.rejected: Accessibility status is necessary but not sufficient for channel activation.

references.label: NHS digital service manual layout
references.borrowed: Narrow and mobile-first embedded layouts must keep blockers and source trace visible.
references.rejected: Route-specific style choices remain governed by local token contracts.

references.label: WCAG 2.2 quick reference
references.borrowed: The matrix and rail are rendered with semantic controls, non-colour-only status text, and keyboard-visible focus.
references.rejected: WCAG evidence does not change row authority state.

references.label: Playwright locators and ARIA snapshots
references.borrowed: Browser verification uses role-first locators where practical, ARIA snapshots, isolated contexts, screenshots, and deterministic route states.
references.rejected: Browser green paths cannot reconcile Phase 7 as complete without source tuple authority.
